journal_entry.description: Issue common stock for cash ($100 par value, $80 additional paid-in capital)
journal_entry.rationale: Stock issuance increases equity through two components: (1) Common Stock at par value ($100), representing the legal stated value of the shares, and (2) Additional Paid-In Capital ($80), representing the premium received above par. Cash increases by the total proceeds ($180). The credit tuple indicates 2 equity increases (b=2), which correctly reflects the two equity accounts credited: Common Stock and APIC. This follows IFRS standard for share capital presentation where proceeds are split between par value and premium.

output.reason: Issuing common stock means the company receives cash. Cash is an asset, so the debit increases the asset (Cash). There is 1 debit line: Cash increases by $180.

debit_classifier.reason: Issuing common stock means the company receives cash. Cash is an asset, so the debit increases the asset (Cash). There is 1 debit line: Cash increases by $180.

output.reason: Issuing common stock creates two equity increases: (1) Common Stock at par value = equity increase, and (2) Additional Paid-In Capital (APIC) for the amount above par = equity increase. Two separate credit lines, both increasing equity accounts.

credit_classifier.reason: Issuing common stock creates two equity increases: (1) Common Stock at par value = equity increase, and (2) Additional Paid-In Capital (APIC) for the amount above par = equity increase. Two separate credit lines, both increasing equity accounts.

output.description: Issue common stock for cash ($100 par value, $80 additional paid-in capital)
output.rationale: Stock issuance increases equity through two components: (1) Common Stock at par value ($100), representing the legal stated value of the shares, and (2) Additional Paid-In Capital ($80), representing the premium received above par. Cash increases by the total proceeds ($180). The credit tuple indicates 2 equity increases (b=2), which correctly reflects the two equity accounts credited: Common Stock and APIC. This follows IFRS standard for share capital presentation where proceeds are split between par value and premium.

output.reason: Entry correctly records common stock issuance for cash. Balance verified: debits $180 = credits $180 ($100 + $80). Accounts are appropriate: Cash (asset) debited for total proceeds, Common Stock credited at par value $100, and Additional Paid-In Capital credited for premium $80. All amounts match transaction description exactly. Directionality correct: Cash debit increases asset, both equity credits increase equity accounts. Complete - no missing lines. Follows IFRS presentation for share capital split between par and premium.
